content.claim: Polish survey data (IPSOS/OKO.press 2016) indicates that support for the 2017 reform reverting away from gimnazjums correlated with respondents' own schooling experience, with 18-29-year-olds (who had attended gimnazjums) being the only age group opposed to dismantling them, suggesting generational and experiential variation in reform preferences.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: Opinion/preference data, not causal or outcome analysis
Interpreted as evidence of preference correlation but does not establish whether experience-based opposition reflects educational outcomes or other factors (e.g., identity, familiarity)
Single snapshot survey; does not track opinion evolution pre- and post-reversal implementation
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
